project.name: flight_ops_app
project.description: A lightweight POC .NET 8 backend that fetches live aircraft state from the OpenSky Network REST API for a caller-supplied bounding box, and serves it via REST endpoints and a SignalR push hub, for a companion Flutter mobile app. | Live Flight Ops Dashboard mobile frontend — a Flutter map/list dashboard for live aircraft positions, consuming a companion .NET REST/SignalR API.

nodes.name: FlightOps.Api.csproj
nodes.summary: MSBuild project file for the ASP.NET Core Web API host; targets .NET 8, references the FlightOps.Infrastructure project, and pulls in FluentValidation and Swashbuckle (Swagger) packages.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web SDK style project format with nullable reference types and implicit usings enabled.

nodes.name: appsettings.json
nodes.summary: Base runtime configuration for the API: default logging levels, the OpenSky Network auth token URL and API base URL, the token refresh margin, and the broadcast interval used by the SignalR push loop.

nodes.name: appsettings.Development.json
nodes.summary: Development-environment configuration override that raises default logging verbosity; ASP.NET Core merges it on top of appsettings.json when running under the Development environment.

nodes.name: appsettings.secrets.json
nodes.summary: Local, credential-bearing overlay for the OpenSky Network OAuth client id and secret used to authenticate against the OpenSky token endpoint; layered onto appsettings.json's OpenSkySettings section and expected to be kept out of source control.
nodes.languageNotes: Holds real credential-shaped values (client id/secret) rather than placeholders, so it should never be committed or echoed verbatim.

nodes.name: flight-ops-api.postman_collection.json
nodes.summary: Postman collection with example requests exercising the FlightOps REST endpoints, letting a developer manually verify aircraft snapshot, live-interval, and aircraft-detail routes against a running instance.

nodes.name: FlightOps.sln
nodes.summary: Visual Studio solution wiring together the Domain, Infrastructure, and Api class libraries plus the test project into one buildable unit.

nodes.name: AircraftController.cs
nodes.summary: REST controller exposing aircraft snapshot, live-interval override, and single-aircraft lookup endpoints, validating bounding-box queries before delegating to the snapshot provider and OpenSky client.

nodes.name: AircraftController
nodes.summary: ASP.NET Core API controller mounted at /aircraft that validates bounding-box requests, reads the optional live-interval header, and serves snapshot and single-aircraft lookups.

nodes.name: GetAircraft
nodes.summary: Validates the bounding-box query, optionally applies a caller-supplied live-interval override, and returns the current aircraft snapshot for that area.

nodes.name: SetLiveInterval
nodes.summary: Reads and validates the X-Live-Interval-Seconds header and updates the shared live polling interval.

nodes.name: GetAircraftDetail
nodes.summary: Validates an ICAO24 hex address and looks up a single aircraft's live state directly from OpenSky.

nodes.name: ActiveBoundingBoxRegistry.cs
nodes.summary: Thread-safe in-memory registry tracking which SignalR connections are subscribed to which bounding-box groups and each group's own broadcast timer, using reference counting to know when a group has no more subscribers.

nodes.name: IActiveBoundingBoxRegistry
nodes.summary: Contract for registering/unregistering hub connections to bounding-box groups and tracking each group's independent broadcast due-time.

nodes.name: ActiveBoundingBoxRegistry
nodes.summary: ConcurrentDictionary-backed implementation that reference-counts subscribers per group and stamps a per-group last-broadcast timestamp so groups broadcast on independent clocks.

nodes.name: Unregister
nodes.summary: Removes a connection from its group and, once a group's subscriber count drops to zero, cleans up all per-group tracking state.

nodes.name: AircraftBroadcastService.cs
nodes.summary: Background service that ticks once a second and pushes fresh aircraft snapshots to each subscribed SignalR group whenever that group's own broadcast interval has elapsed.

nodes.name: AircraftBroadcastService
nodes.summary: Hosted BackgroundService driving periodic per-group aircraft broadcasts over SignalR based on the shared live-interval setting and each group's own due timer.

nodes.name: ExecuteAsync
nodes.summary: Main polling loop: waits one second, then broadcasts to every active group whose interval has elapsed and marks it as freshly broadcast.

nodes.name: AircraftHub.cs
nodes.summary: SignalR hub handling client subscriptions to a bounding box: registers the connection in the active-group registry, joins the SignalR group, and returns an immediate snapshot as the RPC response.

nodes.name: AircraftHub
nodes.summary: SignalR Hub exposing a Subscribe RPC that registers the caller's bounding box and group membership, plus disconnect cleanup.

nodes.name: Subscribe
nodes.summary: Registers the caller's bounding box in the group registry, adds the SignalR connection to the corresponding group, and returns the current snapshot directly as the RPC result to avoid a race with the broadcast loop.

nodes.name: BroadcastSettings.cs
nodes.summary: Configuration POCO binding the 'Broadcast' section, holding the default broadcast Interval used to seed the shared live-interval setting at startup.

nodes.name: BroadcastSettings
nodes.summary: Options-pattern settings class exposing the configured broadcast Interval, bound from the 'Broadcast' configuration section.

nodes.name: OpenSkyExceptionHandler.cs
nodes.summary: Global IExceptionHandler that converts an OpenSkyRateLimitedException into a 503 ProblemDetails response instead of letting it surface as an unhandled error.

nodes.name: OpenSkyExceptionHandler
nodes.summary: ASP.NET Core exception handler translating OpenSky rate-limit exceptions into a 503 Service Unavailable problem response.

nodes.name: TryHandleAsync
nodes.summary: Checks whether the thrown exception is an OpenSkyRateLimitedException and, if so, writes a 503 ProblemDetails response and reports the exception as handled.

nodes.name: Program.cs
nodes.summary: Composition root for the FlightOps API: wires the OpenSky HTTP client, SignalR hub and broadcast service, controllers, validation, and Swagger, then starts the Kestrel host.

nodes.name: Program
nodes.summary: Empty partial class marker exposing the top-level Program type so WebApplicationFactory<Program> can bootstrap the API host in integration tests.

nodes.name: launchSettings.json
nodes.summary: ASP.NET Core launch profile configuration defining local development URLs, ports, and environment variables for running the Api host.

nodes.name: BoundingBoxRequest.cs
nodes.summary: Query-string DTO carrying the four corner coordinates (LaMin/LoMin/LaMax/LoMax) of a caller-requested bounding box.

nodes.name: BoundingBoxRequest
nodes.summary: Plain DTO with LaMin, LoMin, LaMax, LoMax properties bound from the aircraft snapshot endpoint's query string.

nodes.name: BoundingBoxRequestValidator.cs
nodes.summary: FluentValidation validator enforcing valid latitude/longitude ranges and that each bounding box's min is strictly less than its max.

nodes.name: BoundingBoxRequestValidator
nodes.summary: AbstractValidator<BoundingBoxRequest> checking coordinate ranges and min-less-than-max invariants for both axes.

nodes.name: BoundingBoxRequestValidator
nodes.summary: Declares the coordinate-range and min-less-than-max validation rules for a bounding box request.

nodes.name: Aircraft.cs
nodes.summary: Core domain record representing a single aircraft's live state — position, altitude, velocity, heading, ground status, and last-update time.

nodes.name: Aircraft
nodes.summary: Immutable record describing one aircraft's ICAO24 id, callsign, position, motion, and last-update timestamp as returned to API and SignalR consumers.

nodes.name: BoundingBox.cs
nodes.summary: Core domain record for a geographic bounding box, with grid-rounding for cache-key normalization and a derived CacheKey string.

nodes.name: BoundingBox
nodes.summary: Immutable record with LaMin/LoMin/LaMax/LoMax bounds, a RoundToGrid method that snaps a box to a coarser grid, and a CacheKey string used to group requests for caching.

nodes.name: FlightOps.Domain.csproj
nodes.summary: Minimal class-library project file for the Domain layer, targeting net8.0 with nullable reference types and implicit usings enabled and no external package dependencies.

nodes.name: LiveIntervalSettings.cs
nodes.summary: Thread-safe, runtime-mutable holder for the shared SignalR broadcast interval, allowing any client's interval change request to affect every connected client.

nodes.name: ILiveIntervalSettings
nodes.summary: Contract for reading and updating the single shared live-broadcast interval.

nodes.name: LiveIntervalSettings
nodes.summary: Lock-guarded singleton implementation of ILiveIntervalSettings holding one process-wide TimeSpan interval.

nodes.name: OpenSkySettings.cs
nodes.summary: Configuration POCO binding OpenSky API connection details — token URL, API base URL, and OAuth client credentials.

nodes.name: OpenSkySettings
nodes.summary: Options-pattern settings exposing the OpenSky token URL, API base URL, client id, and client secret bound from the 'OpenSky' configuration section.

nodes.name: TokenSettings.cs
nodes.summary: Configuration POCO controlling how much lead time is used when refreshing the cached OpenSky OAuth token before it expires.

nodes.name: TokenSettings
nodes.summary: Options-pattern settings exposing the configured RefreshMargin used to pre-emptively refresh the OpenSky access token.

nodes.name: FlightOps.Infrastructure.csproj
nodes.summary: Class-library project file for the Infrastructure layer, referencing FlightOps.Domain and pulling in Memory Caching, Logging Abstractions, and Options packages used by the OpenSky client and settings classes.

nodes.name: IOpenSkyClient.cs
nodes.summary: Abstraction over the OpenSky Network REST API, exposing bounding-box state queries and single-aircraft lookups by ICAO24.

nodes.name: IOpenSkyClient
nodes.summary: Contract with GetStatesAsync (bounding-box query) and GetStateByIcao24Async (single-aircraft lookup) methods against the OpenSky API.

nodes.name: IOpenSkyTokenClient.cs
nodes.summary: Abstraction for fetching a fresh OAuth access token from OpenSky's token endpoint.

nodes.name: IOpenSkyTokenClient
nodes.summary: Contract exposing FetchTokenAsync, returning a new OpenSkyToken from OpenSky's OAuth token endpoint.

nodes.name: OpenSkyClient.cs
nodes.summary: HTTP client implementation querying the OpenSky /api/states/all endpoint for a bounding box or a single ICAO24 aircraft, mapping raw JSON state vectors into domain Aircraft records and surfacing rate-limit responses as a typed exception.

nodes.name: OpenSkyClient
nodes.summary: IOpenSkyClient implementation that issues OpenSky state queries over an injected HttpClient, parses the JSON response, and logs remaining API quota from response headers.

nodes.name: GetStatesAsync
nodes.summary: Queries OpenSky for all aircraft states within a bounding box and maps each JSON state vector into an Aircraft record.

nodes.name: GetStateByIcao24Async
nodes.summary: Queries OpenSky for a single aircraft's state by ICAO24 address and maps the first result, or null if none found.

nodes.name: SendAsync
nodes.summary: Issues the underlying HTTP GET, throwing OpenSkyRateLimitedException on a 429 response and logging the remaining rate-limit quota from response headers.

nodes.name: OpenSkyRateLimitedException.cs
nodes.summary: Marker exception signaling that the OpenSky API responded with a 429 Too Many Requests, caught upstream to return a 503 to API callers.

nodes.name: OpenSkyRateLimitedException
nodes.summary: Custom exception thrown when OpenSky returns a 429 rate-limit response; handled by OpenSkyExceptionHandler to produce a 503.

nodes.name: OpenSkyStateMapper.cs
nodes.summary: Static mapper translating OpenSky's raw JSON state-vector array format into strongly-typed Aircraft domain records.

nodes.name: OpenSkyStateMapper
nodes.summary: Static class with a Map method converting an OpenSky JSON state-vector element into an Aircraft record, including nullable numeric field handling.

nodes.name: Map
nodes.summary: Reads each positional field of an OpenSky JSON state vector and constructs the corresponding Aircraft record, converting the Unix timestamp to a DateTimeOffset.

nodes.name: OpenSkyToken.cs
nodes.summary: Domain record pairing an OpenSky OAuth access token with its expiry time, used by the token client and its caching consumer.

nodes.name: OpenSkyToken
nodes.summary: Immutable record holding an OpenSky AccessToken string and its ExpiresAt timestamp.

nodes.name: OpenSkyTokenClient.cs
nodes.summary: Fetches a new OAuth2 access token from the OpenSky Network token endpoint using client-credentials form POST, mapping the raw JSON response into an OpenSkyToken with a computed expiry.

nodes.name: OpenSkyTokenClient
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around HttpClient that requests and parses a bearer token from OpenSky's OAuth2 token endpoint.

nodes.name: FetchTokenAsync
nodes.summary: POSTs client-credentials form data to the OpenSky token endpoint and returns an OpenSkyToken with its computed absolute expiry timestamp.

nodes.name: OpenSkyTokenProvider.cs
nodes.summary: DelegatingHandler that attaches a valid bearer token to every outgoing OpenSky request, caching the token in memory and refreshing it via a semaphore-guarded call to OpenSkyTokenClient when it nears expiry.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SemaphoreSlim to serialize token refreshes so concurrent requests don't trigger duplicate token fetches.

nodes.name: OpenSkyTokenProvider
nodes.summary: HTTP DelegatingHandler that transparently injects a cached, auto-refreshed bearer token into every request sent through the pipeline.

nodes.name: GetValidTokenAsync
nodes.summary: Returns the cached token if still valid, otherwise acquires a lock and fetches a fresh token from OpenSkyTokenClient, double-checking validity to avoid redundant refreshes.

nodes.name: AircraftSnapshot.cs
nodes.summary: Small record representing a point-in-time snapshot of aircraft states returned to API and hub consumers.

nodes.name: DirectSnapshotProvider.cs
nodes.summary: The single registered IAircraftSnapshotProvider implementation: calls the OpenSky client directly on every request with no caching layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately the only IAircraftSnapshotProvider implementation currently wired into DI; a previously-explored cached alternative was removed.

nodes.name: DirectSnapshotProvider
nodes.summary: Implements IAircraftSnapshotProvider by delegating straight to IOpenSkyClient.GetStatesAsync with no caching.

nodes.name: GetSnapshotAsync
nodes.summary: Fetches live aircraft states for the given bounding box from the OpenSky client and wraps them in an AircraftSnapshot.

nodes.name: IAircraftSnapshotProvider.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the swappable-provider contract for fetching an AircraftSnapshot for a given bounding box, allowing the snapshot-fetching strategy to be swapped without changing callers.

nodes.name: IAircraftSnapshotProvider
nodes.summary: Single-method interface for retrieving an aircraft snapshot within a bounding box; currently implemented only by DirectSnapshotProvider.

nodes.name: AircraftControllerTests.cs
nodes.summary: Integration tests spinning up the API via WebApplicationFactory with substituted snapshot provider, OpenSky client and live-interval settings, covering the aircraft list/detail endpoints and the live-interval header behavior.

nodes.name: AircraftControllerTests
nodes.summary: NUnit fixture covering AircraftController's GET aircraft/detail endpoints and the X-Live-Interval header handling, using an in-memory WebApplicationFactory with NSubstitute test doubles.

nodes.name: BoundingBoxTests.cs
nodes.summary: Unit tests for BoundingBox's grid-rounding and cache-key formatting logic.

nodes.name: BoundingBoxTests
nodes.summary: Verifies BoundingBox.RoundToGrid snaps to grid boundaries and CacheKey formats coordinates to two decimal places.

nodes.name: ExceptionHandlingTests.cs
nodes.summary: Integration test verifying that an OpenSkyRateLimitedException thrown by the snapshot provider is translated into a 503 HTTP response by the API's exception handling.

nodes.name: ExceptionHandlingTests
nodes.summary: Confirms the API returns HTTP 503 when the snapshot provider throws OpenSkyRateLimitedException, using a substituted provider and WebApplicationFactory.

nodes.name: FlightOps.Api.Tests.csproj
nodes.summary: Project file for the NUnit test suite, wiring NUnit, NSubstitute, and ASP.NET Core test-host packages plus a project reference to the API project under test.

nodes.name: ActiveBoundingBoxRegistryTests.cs
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the SignalR active-bounding-box group registry: registration, unregistration, connection reassignment between groups, and the IsDue/MarkBroadcast timing gate.

nodes.name: ActiveBoundingBoxRegistryTests
nodes.summary: Covers group membership tracking (register/unregister/move) and per-group broadcast-due timing logic of the active bounding-box registry.

nodes.name: AircraftBroadcastServiceTests.cs
nodes.summary: Unit test verifying the broadcast service pushes a fetched snapshot to each registered SignalR group via IHubContext.

nodes.name: AircraftBroadcastServiceTests
nodes.summary: Verifies BroadcastOnceAsync sends the current snapshot to every registered group's client proxy exactly once.

nodes.name: AircraftHubTests.cs
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the SignalR AircraftHub covering group-name formatting, client subscription/group registration, and cleanup on disconnect.

nodes.name: AircraftHubTests
nodes.summary: Verifies AircraftHub's group naming, Subscribe registration behavior, and registry unregistration on OnDisconnectedAsync.

nodes.name: DirectSnapshotProviderTests.cs
nodes.summary: Unit test confirming DirectSnapshotProvider calls the OpenSky client on every invocation with no caching between calls.

nodes.name: DirectSnapshotProviderTests
nodes.summary: Asserts DirectSnapshotProvider.GetSnapshotAsync hits the underlying client twice for two sequential calls, proving the absence of caching.

nodes.name: OpenSkyClientTests.cs
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the OpenSky REST client covering state mapping, 429 rate-limit exception translation, and correct URL-encoding of the icao24 parameter, using a stubbed HttpMessageHandler.

nodes.name: OpenSkyClientTests
nodes.summary: Verifies OpenSkyClient's state mapping, rate-limit exception handling on HTTP 429, and icao24 URL encoding via a stub/capturing HttpMessageHandler.

nodes.name: OpenSkyStateMapperTests.cs
nodes.summary: Unit tests for OpenSkyStateMapper.Map, covering a fully-populated state vector and one with null altitude and on-ground true.

nodes.name: OpenSkyStateMapperTests
nodes.summary: Asserts OpenSkyStateMapper correctly maps raw OpenSky JSON state vectors into domain Aircraft fields, including null/edge-case handling.

nodes.name: OpenSkyTokenClientTests.cs
nodes.summary: Unit test verifying OpenSkyTokenClient parses the access token and computes a future expiry from a stubbed token-endpoint response.

nodes.name: OpenSkyTokenClientTests
nodes.summary: Confirms FetchTokenAsync returns the parsed access token with an expiry timestamp in the future, using a stub HttpMessageHandler.

nodes.name: OpenSkyTokenProviderTests.cs
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the OpenSkyTokenProvider DelegatingHandler covering bearer-token attachment on first request, cache reuse when the token is fresh, and refresh when it nears expiry.

nodes.name: OpenSkyTokenProviderTests
nodes.summary: Verifies token attachment, caching, and near-expiry refresh behavior of OpenSkyTokenProvider using a substituted token client and a recording inner HTTP handler.

nodes.name: ProgramSmokeTests.cs
nodes.summary: Smoke test asserting the DI container resolves IAircraftSnapshotProvider to the DirectSnapshotProvider implementation, guarding the swappable-provider wiring in Program.cs.

nodes.name: ProgramSmokeTests
nodes.summary: Boots the app via WebApplicationFactory and asserts the resolved IAircraftSnapshotProvider is a DirectSnapshotProvider instance.

nodes.name: BoundingBoxRequestValidatorTests.cs
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the FluentValidation validator on BoundingBoxRequest, covering a valid box, LaMin >= LaMax, and out-of-range latitude.

nodes.name: BoundingBoxRequestValidatorTests
nodes.summary: Verifies the bounding-box request validator accepts valid coordinates and rejects invalid latitude ordering and out-of-range values.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Default Flutter project README with boilerplate getting-started links; not yet customized with project-specific setup or architecture notes.

nodes.name: analysis_options.yaml
nodes.summary: Dart analyzer/linter configuration that extends the standard flutter_lints rule set and enforces single-quoted strings.

nodes.name: pubspec.yaml
nodes.summary: Flutter package manifest for flight_ops_app declaring the app's dependencies (flutter_bloc, go_router, flutter_map, dio, signalr_netcore, get_it/injectable) and dev tooling (build_runner, bloc_test, mocktail) that define the app's clean-architecture, BLoC-based, real-time dashboard stack.
nodes.languageNotes: Pins signalr_netcore for real-time SignalR updates alongside dio for REST, and injectable/get_it for DI code generation — reflects a layered Flutter architecture rather than a simple single-page app.

nodes.name: .DS_Store
nodes.summary: macOS Finder metadata file auto-generated for folder view settings; not part of the application logic.

nodes.name: .metadata
nodes.summary: Flutter tooling metadata file tracking the SDK channel, framework revision, and per-platform migration state used by `flutter` CLI commands.

nodes.name: flight_ops_app.iml
nodes.summary: IntelliJ/Android Studio module descriptor defining the Flutter module facet and library dependencies for IDE integration.

nodes.name: api_config.dart
nodes.summary: Centralizes the API base URL, REST paths, and query-parameter names for the aircraft API, resolved via a compile-time `API_BASE_URL` environment define.

nodes.name: injection.config.dart
nodes.summary: Auto-generated injectable/get_it registration file wiring the app's DI container: data sources, repositories, and blocs.
nodes.languageNotes: Generated by the injectable_generator build_runner package from @injectable/@LazySingleton annotations elsewhere in the codebase.

nodes.name: injection.dart
nodes.summary: Public entry point that triggers get_it dependency registration by calling the generated `init` extension.

nodes.name: register_module.dart
nodes.summary: Injectable module providing third-party singletons (Dio HTTP client, SignalR hub URL, SharedPreferences instance) that can't be constructor-injected directly.

nodes.name: app_router.dart
nodes.summary: Builds the app's go_router configuration with a StatefulShellRoute defining the Map, Aircraft List, and Settings tabs plus the aircraft detail route.

nodes.name: app_colors.dart
nodes.summary: Immutable color palette model for the dashboard's dark/light themes, with `copyWith` and `lerp` support for animated theme transitions.

nodes.name: app_strings.dart
nodes.summary: Static repository of user-facing copy strings (labels, messages, section titles) used throughout the UI.

nodes.name: app_text_styles.dart
nodes.summary: Static factory of named TextStyle presets (labels, captions, badges, card titles) built on the Barlow font family for consistent typography.

nodes.name: app_theme.dart
nodes.summary: Builds Flutter ThemeData for dark and light modes from AppColors, applying Google Fonts (Barlow/IBM Plex Mono) across the text theme.

nodes.name: aircraft_remote_data_source.dart
nodes.summary: Dio-based REST client for the aircraft API: fetches live snapshots and aircraft detail, updates the live-interval setting, and maps Dio exceptions to domain Failures.

nodes.name: aircraft_dto.dart
nodes.summary: JSON-serializable DTO for a single aircraft position, converting REST fields into the domain Aircraft entity.

nodes.name: aircraft_dto.g.dart
nodes.summary: Generated json_serializable code providing AircraftDto's fromJson factory implementation.

nodes.name: aircraft_snapshot_dto.dart
nodes.summary: JSON-serializable DTO wrapping a list of aircraft plus a staleness flag, converting the REST snapshot response into the domain AircraftSnapshot.

nodes.name: aircraft_snapshot_dto.g.dart
nodes.summary: Generated json_serializable code providing AircraftSnapshotDto's fromJson factory implementation.

nodes.name: aircraft_repository_impl.dart
nodes.summary: Domain repository implementation that switches between SignalR push updates and REST polling for aircraft snapshots based on the current live-update mode, and delegates detail fetch and live-interval updates to the remote data source.

nodes.name: settings_repository_impl.dart
nodes.summary: SharedPreferences-backed settings repository managing live-update mode, theme mode, and live-interval preferences with reactive stream broadcasts on change.

nodes.name: aircraft_signalr_data_source.dart
nodes.summary: SignalR client managing a lazily-established hub connection with auto-reconnect, subscribing to live aircraft position pushes for a given bounding box.

nodes.name: aircraft_snapshot.dart
nodes.summary: Domain entity representing a point-in-time snapshot of tracked aircraft plus a flag indicating whether the data is stale.

nodes.name: aircraft.dart
nodes.summary: Core domain entity representing a single tracked aircraft's position, velocity, heading, and identification fields.

nodes.name: bounding_box.dart
nodes.summary: Domain value object representing a geographic bounding box (min/max latitude and longitude) used to scope aircraft queries.

nodes.name: failure.dart
nodes.summary: Sealed Failure hierarchy (network, server, not-found, unknown) representing the domain's typed error taxonomy for repository operations.

nodes.name: repository_exception.dart
nodes.summary: Exception wrapper carrying a domain Failure so repository-layer errors can be thrown and caught with typed context.

nodes.name: ApiConfig
nodes.summary: Static configuration class exposing the API base URL, REST paths, and helper methods for building the SignalR hub URL and aircraft detail path.

nodes.name: GetItInjectableX
nodes.summary: Generated extension on GetIt registering all injectable data sources, repositories, and blocs into the DI container.

nodes.name: RegisterModule
nodes.summary: Injectable module class providing Dio, the SignalR hub URL, and SharedPreferences as injectable dependencies.

nodes.name: AppColors
nodes.summary: Immutable palette of themed colors (canvas, panel, accent, danger, etc.) with copyWith and lerp for smooth theme animation.

nodes.name: AppStrings
nodes.summary: Static collection of user-facing string constants for labels and messages across the dashboard UI.

nodes.name: AppTextStyles
nodes.summary: Static factory of named text style presets (section labels, captions, badges, card titles) parameterized by color.

nodes.name: AppTheme
nodes.summary: Builds dark and light ThemeData instances from AppColors, applying Barlow/IBM Plex Mono Google Fonts across the text theme.

nodes.name: AircraftRemoteDataSource
nodes.summary: Injectable Dio-based client fetching aircraft snapshots and details from the REST API, mapping Dio errors into domain failures.

nodes.name: AircraftDto
nodes.summary: JSON-serializable DTO mirroring the REST aircraft payload, with a toDomain mapper producing the Aircraft entity.

nodes.name: AircraftSnapshotDto
nodes.summary: JSON-serializable DTO wrapping a list of AircraftDto plus a stale flag, mapped via toDomain to AircraftSnapshot.

nodes.name: AircraftRepositoryImpl
nodes.summary: Repository implementation combining the SignalR and REST data sources, switching update strategy based on settings and exposing snapshot/detail/live-interval operations.

nodes.name: SettingsRepositoryImpl
nodes.summary: SharedPreferences-backed implementation of the settings repository, caching current values and broadcasting changes via streams.

nodes.name: AircraftSignalRDataSource
nodes.summary: Manages a SignalR hub connection with auto-reconnect and re-subscription, exposing a stream of live aircraft snapshot updates for a bounding box.

nodes.name: AircraftSnapshot
nodes.summary: Domain entity pairing a list of aircraft with a staleness flag for freshness-aware UI rendering.

nodes.name: Aircraft
nodes.summary: Domain entity holding a tracked aircraft's identity, position, velocity, heading, and last-update timestamp.

nodes.name: BoundingBox
nodes.summary: Value object describing a geographic bounding box used to scope aircraft snapshot queries.

nodes.name: Failure
nodes.summary: Sealed base class defining the domain's typed error taxonomy (NetworkFailure, ServerFailure, NotFoundFailure, UnknownFailure) with a human-readable message.

nodes.name: RepositoryException
nodes.summary: Exception type wrapping a domain Failure for throwing and catching typed repository errors.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Generated DI bootstrap function registering all data sources, repositories, and blocs with get_it in dependency order.

nodes.name: buildAppRouter
nodes.summary: Constructs the GoRouter with a stateful shell hosting Map, Aircraft List, and Settings tabs, plus a standalone aircraft detail route.

nodes.name: copyWith
nodes.summary: Returns a new AppColors with selected fields overridden, defaulting to the current instance's values.

nodes.name: lerp
nodes.summary: Linearly interpolates between this and another AppColors instance for smooth animated theme transitions.

nodes.name: _themeFor
nodes.summary: Builds a ThemeData for a given color palette and brightness, applying the Barlow text theme and Material 3 color scheme.

nodes.name: fetchSnapshot
nodes.summary: Fetches the current aircraft snapshot for a bounding box and live-interval via REST GET, mapping Dio errors to domain failures.

nodes.name: updateLiveInterval
nodes.summary: Posts the desired live-update polling interval to the API, translating failures into domain RepositoryExceptions.

nodes.name: fetchDetail
nodes.summary: Fetches detailed information for a single aircraft by ICAO24 code via REST GET.

nodes.name: _mapDioException
nodes.summary: Maps a DioException to the appropriate domain Failure subtype (network, server, or unknown) based on the error type.

nodes.name: toDomain
nodes.summary: Converts the DTO's raw fields into the domain Aircraft entity, parsing the last-update timestamp.

nodes.name: watchSnapshot
nodes.summary: Streams aircraft snapshots for a bounding box, switching between SignalR push updates and REST polling as the live-update mode setting changes.

nodes.name: _pollSnapshot
nodes.summary: Repeatedly fetches aircraft snapshots via REST on a timer until cancelled, forwarding results or errors to the caller.

nodes.name: parseAircraftUpdateArguments
nodes.summary: Parses the raw SignalR hub method arguments into an AircraftSnapshot, throwing a FormatException on malformed input.

nodes.name: _connect
nodes.summary: Builds and starts the SignalR hub connection with automatic reconnect, wiring the aircraft update handler and re-subscribing on reconnect.

nodes.name: watchSnapshot
nodes.summary: Ensures the hub connection is established, invokes the subscribe RPC for a bounding box, and returns the stream of live aircraft updates.

nodes.name: aircraft_repository.dart
nodes.summary: Abstract repository interface for live aircraft data — streaming region snapshots, on-demand detail lookups, and adjusting the backend's SignalR broadcast interval.

nodes.name: settings_repository.dart
nodes.summary: Abstract repository interface for persisted user settings — live-update mode, theme mode, and polling interval, each exposed as a current value plus a change stream.

nodes.name: live_update_mode.dart
nodes.summary: Single enum defining the two live-update strategies (standard polling vs realtime dead-reckoning) used across settings and the map.

nodes.name: main.dart
nodes.summary: App entry point that initializes dependency injection, notifies the backend of the persisted polling interval, and boots the widget tree with a restart mechanism for applying settings changes.

nodes.name: active_region_bloc.dart
nodes.summary: Bloc that watches the aircraft repository's snapshot stream for the current map/list viewport, restarting the subscription on each region change and preserving stale data across errors.

nodes.name: active_region_event.dart
nodes.summary: Event definitions (part file) for ActiveRegionBloc — a single event carrying the updated bounding box for the active viewport.

nodes.name: active_region_state.dart
nodes.summary: State definitions (part file) for ActiveRegionBloc, modeling initial/loading/loaded/error variants with stale-snapshot retention on error.

nodes.name: aircraft_detail_page.dart
nodes.summary: Aircraft detail screen composing altitude/velocity/heading cards and record rows from a per-instance AircraftDetailBloc, shared between the full-page route and the map's bottom sheet.

nodes.name: aircraft_detail_bloc.dart
nodes.summary: Factory-scoped bloc that fetches a single aircraft's detail record by ICAO24 from the repository and maps success/not-found/error into state.

nodes.name: aircraft_detail_event.dart
nodes.summary: Event definitions (part file) for AircraftDetailBloc — a single request-detail event.

nodes.name: aircraft_detail_state.dart
nodes.summary: State definitions (part file) for AircraftDetailBloc covering loading, loaded, not-found, and error variants.

nodes.name: data_card.dart
nodes.summary: Reusable labeled-value card widget used on the aircraft detail screen to display altitude, velocity, and heading readouts.

nodes.name: freshness_indicator.dart
nodes.summary: Self-ticking widget showing a blinking dot plus a relative 'Updated Xs/m/h ago' label, refreshed every second via an internal Timer.

nodes.name: AircraftRepository
nodes.summary: Repository contract for streaming region snapshots, fetching single-aircraft detail, and retuning the backend's live-update interval.

nodes.name: SettingsRepository
nodes.summary: Repository contract exposing current values and change streams for live-update mode, theme mode, and polling interval.

nodes.name: RestartWidget
nodes.summary: Wraps the app in a keyed subtree so restartApp can force a full rebuild with a fresh DI container after settings that require a restart change.

nodes.name: _RestartWidgetState
nodes.summary: Holds the UniqueKey that, when replaced, forces Flutter to discard and recreate the wrapped subtree.

nodes.name: FlightOpsApp
nodes.summary: Root MaterialApp.router widget wiring the global SettingsBloc's theme mode into the app-wide light/dark theme and the go_router configuration.

nodes.name: ActiveRegionBloc
nodes.summary: Core region-data bloc: restartable event handling ensures only the latest viewport's snapshot stream is subscribed, retaining prior data during refetch or error.

nodes.name: ActiveRegionState
nodes.summary: Sealed base state exposing a computed bbox getter that pattern-matches across all four state variants.

nodes.name: AircraftDetailPage
nodes.summary: Route-level page widget that provisions a per-instance AircraftDetailBloc scoped to the requested ICAO24 and renders the detail view.

nodes.name: AircraftDetailContent
nodes.summary: Renders the full aircraft detail layout — callsign header, ground/airborne badge, data cards for altitude/velocity/heading, and formatted record rows — with local flight-level, knots, and timestamp formatting helpers.

nodes.name: AircraftDetailBloc
nodes.summary: Factory-scoped bloc (one instance per ICAO24 via @factoryParam) that fetches and re-fetches a single aircraft's detail from the repository.

nodes.name: DataCard
nodes.summary: Bordered label/value/subtext card styled with the app's monospace theme, used for numeric flight readouts.

nodes.name: _FreshnessIndicatorState
nodes.summary: Manages a 1-second periodic Timer that rebuilds the widget to blink the freshness dot and refresh the relative-time label.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: App bootstrap: ensures Flutter bindings, configures DI, notifies the backend of the persisted live interval, then runs the app inside RestartWidget.

nodes.name: _notifyBackendOfPersistedInterval
nodes.summary: One-shot startup call that reads the persisted live-interval setting and pushes it to the backend via the aircraft repository; intentionally not called on every settings change.

nodes.name: restartApp
nodes.summary: Tears down and recreates the DI container, re-notifies the backend of the interval, rebuilds the router, and forces a subtree rebuild — used after settings changes that require a restart.

nodes.name: _onUpdateRegionRequested
nodes.summary: Handles viewport-change events: skips no-op repeats, seeds loading/refreshing state from prior data, and forwards the repository's snapshot stream into loaded/error states with stale-data retention.

nodes.name: _onRequested
nodes.summary: Fetches aircraft detail from the repository and emits loaded, not-found, or error state depending on the result and any thrown RepositoryException.

nodes.name: record_row.dart
nodes.summary: Simple label/value row with monospace value styling, used to list raw aircraft record fields (ICAO24, position, last update, etc.) on the detail screen.

nodes.name: aircraft_list_page.dart
nodes.summary: Aircraft list screen with search filtering, refresh/stale indicators, and tap-through navigation to the detail route, backed by AircraftListBloc.

nodes.name: aircraft_list_bloc.dart
nodes.summary: Bloc that derives the list screen's state from ActiveRegionBloc's stream plus local search-text events, keeping the two concerns orthogonal.

nodes.name: aircraft_list_event.dart
nodes.summary: Event definitions (part file) for AircraftListBloc — search text changes, region updates, and retry requests.

nodes.name: aircraft_list_state.dart
nodes.summary: State definitions (part file) for AircraftListBloc, including the AircraftListLoaded variant's case-insensitive callsign/country search filtering.

nodes.name: aircraft_list_skeleton.dart
nodes.summary: Shimmer-animated loading placeholder mimicking the aircraft list row layout, shown during the initial data fetch.

nodes.name: stale_data_banner.dart
nodes.summary: Warning banner shown above the aircraft list when the displayed snapshot is stale.

nodes.name: map_bloc.dart
nodes.summary: Bloc combining ActiveRegionBloc's region stream with map-only concerns (marker selection, viewport-driven region requests, retry) into map view state.

nodes.name: map_event.dart
nodes.summary: Event definitions (part file) for MapBloc — viewport changes, marker selection, region updates, and retry requests.

nodes.name: map_state.dart
nodes.summary: State definitions (part file) for MapBloc, tracking the current snapshot, refresh status, and selected aircraft.

nodes.name: map_page.dart
nodes.summary: Interactive flutter_map view rendering live aircraft markers with dead-reckoning/interpolation-based position smoothing between polling updates, plus zoom, recenter, and detail bottom-sheet controls.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements client-side motion smoothing: standard mode eases between the last two confirmed reports, realtime mode dead-reckons forward using reported heading/speed, both clamped and projected onto the heading line to avoid backward jitter.

nodes.name: aircraft_marker.dart
nodes.summary: Rotated SVG aircraft icon marker with separate outline/fill layers so the fill color can be tinted per aircraft state (airborne/ground/selected) without losing the outline.

nodes.name: RecordRow
nodes.summary: Two-column row pairing a field label with a monospace value, used for the detail screen's raw record list.

nodes.name: AircraftListPage
nodes.summary: Route-level page widget that provisions AircraftListBloc via DI and renders the list view.

nodes.name: _AircraftListView
nodes.summary: Builds the search field, refresh/stale/error indicators, and the scrollable aircraft list with tap-through navigation to the detail route.

nodes.name: AircraftListBloc
nodes.summary: Combines ActiveRegionBloc's snapshot stream (forwarded via internal events) with local search-text state, retrying region fetches on demand.

nodes.name: AircraftListLoaded
nodes.summary: Loaded state variant holding the full aircraft list plus a visibleAircraft getter that filters by callsign or origin country against the current search query.

nodes.name: _AircraftListSkeletonState
nodes.summary: Builds six shimmer-animated placeholder rows mirroring the real list row's icon/text/trailing layout.

nodes.name: StaleDataBanner
nodes.summary: Bordered warning banner with an icon and stale-snapshot message, styled from the app's stale color token.

nodes.name: MapBloc
nodes.summary: Translates ActiveRegionBloc's region state plus local marker-selection events into MapState, forwarding viewport changes and retries back to ActiveRegionBloc.

nodes.name: MapPage
nodes.summary: Route-level page widget that provisions MapBloc via DI and renders the interactive map view.

nodes.name: _MapViewState
nodes.summary: Owns the FlutterMap controller, per-aircraft motion tracking, and a frame ticker driving continuous position interpolation/dead-reckoning between polling updates, plus zoom/recenter/detail-sheet interactions.

nodes.name: _AircraftMotion
nodes.summary: Per-aircraft motion snapshot storing previous/current positions and timestamps, computed velocity, and the correction blend's start point and duration.

nodes.name: AircraftMarker
nodes.summary: Rotated two-layer SVG marker (tinted fill over a contrast-swapping outline) representing an aircraft's heading and ground/airborne/selected state.

nodes.name: _correctionDurationFor
nodes.summary: Computes how long a position correction should take by dividing the jump distance by the aircraft's reported speed, clamped to a sane min/max so blends never look faster than the plane's real speed.

nodes.name: _telemetryVelocity
nodes.summary: Converts an aircraft's reported speed and heading into latitude/longitude degrees-per-second, used to dead-reckon its position between reports.

nodes.name: _syncMarkerPositions
nodes.summary: Updates the per-aircraft motion map on each new snapshot — pruning aircraft no longer present, seeding first sightings directly, and computing velocity plus correction parameters for subsequent moves.

nodes.name: _positionFor
nodes.summary: Returns an aircraft's current visual position: standard mode eases between the last two confirmed reports (falling back to dead reckoning if the next poll overruns), realtime mode dead-reckons continuously with a correction blend after each new report.

nodes.name: _forwardOnly
nodes.summary: Projects a candidate position onto the aircraft's heading line, discarding any backward or sideways component so corrections never visibly move a marker away from its direction of travel.

nodes.name: _showAircraftDetailSheet
nodes.summary: Opens a draggable bottom sheet hosting a fresh per-instance AircraftDetailBloc and the shared AircraftDetailContent for the tapped marker's aircraft.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Renders the FlutterMap with altitude-sorted, interpolated aircraft markers, an animated loading/live/error status chip, an error overlay with retry, and zoom/recenter floating action buttons.

nodes.name: footer_status.dart
nodes.summary: Splash screen footer showing pulsing status dots synced to the radar animation, indicating background connectivity checks.

nodes.name: FooterStatus
nodes.summary: Widget rendering three dots whose opacity pulses via a sine function of the radar animation, plus a monospace status label.

nodes.name: radar.dart
nodes.summary: Animated radar-sweep graphic (rotating gradient sweep plus expanding pulse rings) used as the centerpiece of the splash screen.

nodes.name: Radar
nodes.summary: Widget combining a rotating sweep gradient, a center icon, and multiple pulse rings (built via the private _ring helper) driven by radar and entrance animations.

nodes.name: wordmark.dart
nodes.summary: Displays the app's eyebrow text and wordmark title on the splash screen with a fade/translate entrance animation.

nodes.name: Wordmark
nodes.summary: Widget rendering the eyebrow label and wordmark text, fading and sliding in based on the entrance animation value.

nodes.name: app_bootstrap_test.dart
nodes.summary: Widget test that boots FlightOpsApp end-to-end with a fake AircraftRepository and mocked SharedPreferences to verify the app renders without crashing.

nodes.name: injection_test.dart
nodes.summary: Verifies that configureDependencies() registers the AircraftRepository and SettingsRepository singletons in the get_it container.

nodes.name: app_theme_test.dart
nodes.summary: Widget test asserting the light and dark ThemeData differ and that the monospace text style resolves to the expected font/color.

nodes.name: aircraft_remote_data_source_test.dart
nodes.summary: Unit tests for AircraftRemoteDataSource covering snapshot/detail fetch success paths, live-interval updates, and mapping of Dio errors to domain failures using a fake Dio HttpClientAdapter.

nodes.name: dio_adapter.dart
nodes.summary: Test-only Dio HttpClientAdapter double that returns canned JSON responses or a connection error for specific paths, used to drive remote data source tests without real HTTP calls.

nodes.name: DioAdapter
nodes.summary: Implements HttpClientAdapter to serve pre-registered canned responses or a DioException by request path, recording the last seen request headers for assertions.

nodes.name: aircraft_dto_test.dart
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying AircraftDto.fromJson parsing and its toDomain mapping into the Aircraft entity.

nodes.name: aircraft_snapshot_dto_test.dart
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying AircraftSnapshotDto.fromJson parsing and its toDomain mapping into the AircraftSnapshot entity, including an empty-list case.

nodes.name: aircraft_repository_impl_test.dart
nodes.summary: Unit tests for AircraftRepositoryImpl covering polling-based watchSnapshot behavior, live-update mode switching, error resilience of the polling stream, and delegation of fetchDetail/updateLiveInterval to the remote data source.

nodes.name: _FakeSettingsRepository
nodes.summary: In-memory fake SettingsRepository exposing a controllable live-update-mode stream and interval, used to drive AircraftRepositoryImpl's polling logic under test.

nodes.name: settings_repository_impl_test.dart
nodes.summary: Unit tests for SettingsRepositoryImpl verifying default values, persistence and stream emission of live-update mode changes, theme mode persistence, and live interval persistence via SharedPreferences.

nodes.name: map_state_overlay.dart
nodes.summary: Thin map-specific wrapper around ConnectivityStateCard used to show loading, error, or empty overlays on top of the flight map.

nodes.name: MapStateOverlay
nodes.summary: Stateless widget that fills the map viewport with a centered ConnectivityStateCard for loading/error/empty states.

nodes.name: settings_bloc.dart
nodes.summary: Bloc that manages theme mode and connection-status settings state, persisting changes through SettingsRepository and forwarding connection updates from ActiveRegionBloc.
nodes.languageNotes: Subscribes to an external ActiveRegionBloc stream and forwards it via an internal event, a common pattern for cross-bloc coordination without exposing the child bloc directly to the UI.

nodes.name: SettingsBloc
nodes.summary: Bloc<SettingsEvent, SettingsState> that handles ThemeModeChanged and SettingsRegionUpdated events, persists theme changes via SettingsRepository, and subscribes to ActiveRegionBloc for live connection status.

nodes.name: settings_event.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the sealed SettingsEvent hierarchy (ThemeModeChanged, SettingsRegionUpdated) consumed by SettingsBloc.

nodes.name: settings_state.dart
nodes.summary: Defines SettingsState and the sealed ConnectionStatus hierarchy (Connecting, Connected, Error) used to render live connection feedback in the settings UI.

nodes.name: settings_page.dart
nodes.summary: Settings screen scaffold that wires a SettingsBloc from the DI container and lays out theme-mode selection, live-update controls, and connection status sections.

nodes.name: _SettingsView
nodes.summary: Private stateless widget rendering the settings page body: theme mode segmented control, LiveUpdatesBlock, and ConnectionIndicator inside a scrollable list.

nodes.name: connection_indicator.dart
nodes.summary: Displays the live SignalR connection status (connecting/connected/error) as a labeled dot with color coding, reading state from SettingsBloc.

nodes.name: ConnectionIndicator
nodes.summary: Widget that switches its visual (spinner, dot color, error text) based on the ConnectionStatus passed in, showing region and message for connected/error states.

nodes.name: live_updates_block.dart
nodes.summary: Settings section letting the user choose live-update mode and polling interval via a slider, with a dirty-tracking Save button that persists changes and restarts the app.

nodes.name: LiveUpdatesBlock
nodes.summary: StatefulWidget accepting the currently applied live-update mode and polling interval as initial values for the settings block.

nodes.name: _LiveUpdatesBlockState
nodes.summary: Tracks pending mode/interval selections, computes a dirty flag, and on save persists both settings through the DI-resolved SettingsRepository before triggering an app restart.

nodes.name: connectivity_state_card.dart
nodes.summary: Reusable card widget presenting an icon, title, subtitle, and optional retry button for loading/error/empty states across map and other screens.

nodes.name: ConnectivityStateCard
nodes.summary: Stateless widget rendering a bordered or plain card with icon, title, subtitle, and an optional retry button wired to an onRetry callback.

nodes.name: live_status_chip.dart
nodes.summary: Compact status chip showing live aircraft count with an animated flip-counter, plus loading/error variants for the top of the dashboard.

nodes.name: LiveStatusChip
nodes.summary: Widget that renders aircraft count via AnimatedFlipCounter with a refreshing spinner, or a loading/error label depending on LiveStatusChipVariant.

nodes.name: shimmer_box.dart
nodes.summary: Shimmer loading placeholder widget plus a reusable ShimmerTickerMixin that manages the shared AnimationController lifecycle for shimmer effects.

nodes.name: ShimmerBox
nodes.summary: Widget that paints a rounded, animated linear-gradient sweep to indicate a loading placeholder for a given width/height.

nodes.name: ShimmerTickerMixin
nodes.summary: Mixin providing a shared shimmer AnimationController with disposal handling, intended to be reused by any State that needs a shimmer effect.

nodes.name: app_shell.dart
nodes.summary: Bottom-navigation shell wrapping go_router's StatefulNavigationShell, rendering animated nav tabs for Map, Settings, and other top-level branches.

nodes.name: AppShell
nodes.summary: Widget hosting a StatefulNavigationShell and a floating pill-style bottom nav bar built from _NavTab entries that call goBranch to switch tabs.

nodes.name: _NavTab
nodes.summary: Private widget rendering a single animated navigation tab (icon plus optional expanding label) that highlights when selected.

nodes.name: splash_page.dart
nodes.summary: Animated splash screen combining Radar, Wordmark, FillBar, and FooterStatus widgets with an entrance animation sequence before app content loads.

nodes.name: _SplashPageState
nodes.summary: Manages the radar and entrance AnimationControllers, listens for entrance completion, and composes the splash layout with a radial gradient background.

nodes.name: fill_bar.dart
nodes.summary: Animated progress bar widget used on the splash screen, filling proportionally to the entrance animation value.

nodes.name: FillBar
nodes.summary: Widget rendering a rounded track with a gradient-filled bar whose width is driven by the entrance animation value.

nodes.name: aircraft_signalr_data_source_test.dart
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the aircraft SignalR data source, verifying that raw update arguments are parsed into aircraft snapshot data with the expected length and field values.

nodes.name: aircraft_snapshot_test.dart
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the AircraftSnapshot entity, verifying value equality based on its aircraft list and timestamp.

nodes.name: aircraft_test.dart
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Aircraft entity, verifying value equality and distinctness across differing field values.

nodes.name: bounding_box_test.dart
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the BoundingBox entity, verifying value equality based on its coordinate bounds.

nodes.name: failure_test.dart
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Failure hierarchy (ServerFailure, NetworkFailure, NotFoundFailure, UnknownFailure), verifying construction and equality semantics between failure subtypes.

nodes.name: active_region_bloc_test.dart
nodes.summary: Bloc tests for ActiveRegionBloc covering region update requests, loading/loaded/error state transitions, debounced repeated requests, and a mocked repository's watchSnapshot stream.

nodes.name: aircraft_detail_bloc_test.dart
nodes.summary: Bloc tests for AircraftDetailBloc covering successful detail loads, not-found results, and repository exceptions surfaced as error states.

nodes.name: aircraft_list_bloc_test.dart
nodes.summary: Bloc tests for AircraftListBloc covering list population from the active region bloc's state stream, search filtering, and error propagation.

nodes.name: map_bloc_test.dart
nodes.summary: Bloc tests for MapBloc covering viewport-change forwarding to the active region bloc, marker selection, and mapped loaded/error states from the region stream.

nodes.name: settings_bloc_test.dart
nodes.summary: Bloc tests for SettingsBloc covering initial state assembly from the settings repository, theme mode changes, and connection state derived from the active region bloc's stream.

edges.description: Restored by assemble-reviewer: merge script tested_by linker misclassified test/*.dart files as production because .dart is absent from its language-specific test-path table, causing genuine prod-to-test pairs to be dropped as prod<->prod.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: ASP.NET Core REST surface for FlightOps: the aircraft controller, request validation/DTOs, exception-handling middleware, and Program.cs composition root that wires the Kestrel host.

layers.name: Real-Time Hub Layer
layers.description: SignalR push infrastructure that tracks per-bounding-box subscriber groups and broadcasts live aircraft snapshots to connected Flutter clients on a timer.

layers.name: Domain Layer
layers.description: Zero-dependency core models shared across the whole solution: the Aircraft state record and the BoundingBox value type with its cache-key normalization.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: External integration code: the OpenSky REST/OAuth client and token provider, configuration POCOs for OpenSky and live-interval settings, and the swappable aircraft-snapshot providers.

layers.name: Configuration & Build
layers.description: Project/build wiring and runtime settings for the three-project .sln layout: csproj files, appsettings layers (base/dev/secrets), the launch profile, the solution file, and the Postman collection used to exercise the API manually.

layers.name: Test Layer
layers.description: NUnit/NSubstitute test suite covering controllers, hubs, OpenSky infrastructure, request validation, domain models, and DI wiring via smoke tests.

layers.name: Core & App Bootstrap
layers.description: App-wide wiring for the flight ops dashboard: DI container setup, go_router navigation config, theme/design tokens, API base-URL config, and the main.dart entry point that boots the widget tree.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Concrete data access for the aircraft API: Dio-based REST data source, SignalR real-time data source, JSON-serializable DTOs, and repository implementations that switch between push (SignalR) and poll (REST) modes, plus SharedPreferences-backed settings persistence.

layers.name: State Management (Blocs)
layers.description: flutter_bloc state-management layer split by feature (active region, aircraft detail, aircraft list, map, settings), each following the bloc/event/state part-file pattern and coordinating around the shared ActiveRegionBloc stream.

layers.name: Presentation UI
layers.description: Screens, feature widgets, the bottom-nav shell, and cross-feature shared components (connectivity states, live status chip, shimmer loaders) that render the map/list dashboard, aircraft detail, settings, and splash screens.

tour.title: Entry Point & Solution Shape
tour.description: Program.cs is the composition root for the whole API: it wires the OpenSky HTTP client (with its OAuth delegating handler), the SignalR hub and broadcast service, controllers, FluentValidation, and Swagger before starting Kestrel. FlightOps.sln ties this together as a deliberately small four-project layout — Domain, Infrastructure, Api, and a test project — with no EF Core, no GraphQL, and no persistence layer, because this is a POC that only needs to proxy live OpenSky data.
tour.languageLesson: ASP.NET Core's minimal hosting model (WebApplication.CreateBuilder) replaces Startup.cs; DI registrations like builder.Services.AddHttpClient<T>().AddHttpMessageHandler<H>() chain a DelegatingHandler onto every outgoing request made through that typed client.

tour.title: Domain Layer: Zero-Dependency Models
tour.description: Aircraft.cs and BoundingBox.cs form the Domain project, which has no package references at all — every other project depends on it, never the reverse. Aircraft is the live-state record (position, altitude, velocity, heading); BoundingBox is the geographic query shape, and it carries grid-rounding logic that produces a normalized CacheKey so nearby, slightly-different boxes can still be treated as the same cache entry later.
tour.languageLesson: C# records give free value equality and immutability, which is exactly what you want for domain value types passed around a request pipeline without risk of accidental mutation.

tour.title: OpenSky OAuth2 Token Handling
tour.description: OpenSkyTokenClient performs the client-credentials POST against OpenSky's token endpoint and maps the JSON reply into an OpenSkyToken (access token + computed expiry), using the client id/secret from OpenSkySettings. OpenSkyTokenProvider is the piece that actually matters at request time: it's a DelegatingHandler that attaches a bearer token to every outgoing OpenSky call, keeps the token cached in memory, and only calls back into OpenSkyTokenClient when the token is within TokenSettings' refresh margin of expiring.
tour.languageLesson: A DelegatingHandler sits transparently in an HttpClient's pipeline; here it's paired with a SemaphoreSlim so concurrent requests near token expiry don't all trigger their own refresh call at once.

tour.title: The OpenSky REST Client
tour.description: OpenSkyClient implements IOpenSkyClient and is the only place in the codebase that actually talks to OpenSky's /api/states/all endpoint, for both bounding-box queries and single-ICAO24 lookups. It delegates the messy work of turning OpenSky's raw JSON state-vector arrays into typed Aircraft records to OpenSkyStateMapper, and turns a 429 response into an OpenSkyRateLimitedException rather than letting a raw HTTP error leak upward.
tour.languageLesson: OpenSkyStateMapper is a static mapper — a pure function with no dependencies, easy to unit test in isolation from any HTTP or DI concerns.

tour.title: Swappable Snapshot Providers
tour.description: IAircraftSnapshotProvider is the interface both the controller (Step 6) and the SignalR hub (Step 7) depend on to get an AircraftSnapshot for a bounding box — neither of them talks to OpenSky directly. DirectSnapshotProvider is the only implementation registered today: it just calls straight through to IOpenSkyClient with no caching, which is fine for a POC but the interface leaves room to drop in a caching provider later without touching either caller.
tour.languageLesson: This is the strategy pattern in miniature: one interface, one swappable implementation, registered once in Program.cs's DI container.

tour.title: REST Endpoints: AircraftController
tour.description: AircraftController is the front door for the Flutter app's plain HTTP calls — aircraft snapshot by bounding box, single-aircraft detail by ICAO24, and a live-interval override endpoint. It binds the query string into a BoundingBoxRequest DTO, validates it (Step 8), and then delegates to the same IAircraftSnapshotProvider and IOpenSkyClient you just saw, rather than duplicating any fetch logic.

tour.title: The Real-Time SignalR Path
tour.description: This is the other half of how Flutter clients get aircraft data: AircraftHub lets a client subscribe to a bounding box, joining a SignalR group and registering itself in ActiveBoundingBoxRegistry, which reference-counts subscribers per group and tracks each group's own broadcast timer. AircraftBroadcastService is a BackgroundService that ticks once a second and pushes a fresh snapshot to any group whose timer is due, using the interval seeded by BroadcastSettings but overridable at runtime through LiveIntervalSettings — the same setting the controller's live-interval endpoint mutates in Step 6.
tour.languageLesson: AircraftBroadcastService's ExecuteAsync loop is the standard .NET BackgroundService pattern for long-running work; LiveIntervalSettings is a thread-safe runtime-mutable singleton rather than a static value, so one client's interval change is visible to every connection sharing the same broadcast loop.

tour.title: Validation and Error Handling
tour.description: BoundingBoxRequestValidator is a FluentValidation validator enforcing that latitude/longitude fall in valid ranges and that each box's min is strictly less than its max, running before AircraftController's logic ever executes. OpenSkyExceptionHandler is the global IExceptionHandler that catches an OpenSkyRateLimitedException anywhere downstream and turns it into a 503 ProblemDetails response instead of a raw unhandled exception — the two together are how the API fails predictably instead of silently.

tour.title: Project Layering in the Build
tour.description: The three csproj files enforce, at build time, the same dependency direction the code follows: FlightOps.Domain.csproj has zero package references, FlightOps.Infrastructure.csproj references Domain plus Memory Caching, Logging Abstractions, and Options packages, and FlightOps.Api.csproj references Infrastructure plus FluentValidation and Swashbuckle. This is the project intentionally staying small — three thin class libraries instead of a heavier layered architecture, appropriate for a POC wrapping one external API.

tour.title: Layered Runtime Configuration
tour.description: appsettings.json holds the base settings from Step 1 and Step 3 — OpenSky's token and API URLs, the token refresh margin, and the default broadcast interval. appsettings.Development.json overrides logging verbosity for local runs, and appsettings.secrets.json layers in the OAuth client id and secret that OpenSkySettings binds to; ASP.NET Core merges all three at startup, and the secrets file is meant to stay out of source control.
tour.languageLesson: ASP.NET Core's configuration providers apply in registration order, each one overriding matching keys from the last — this is how base/environment/secrets layering works without any custom merge logic.

tour.title: Manually Exercising the API
tour.description: launchSettings.json defines the local URLs and ports the Api host listens on when you run it from Visual Studio or `dotnet run`. The Postman collection gives ready-made requests against the aircraft snapshot, live-interval, and aircraft-detail endpoints from Step 6, letting a developer poke the running API by hand without writing any client code first.

tour.title: The Automated Test Suite
tour.description: ProgramSmokeTests guards the DI wiring from Step 1 by asserting IAircraftSnapshotProvider actually resolves to DirectSnapshotProvider. AircraftControllerTests spins up the real API via WebApplicationFactory with substituted dependencies to exercise the REST endpoints end-to-end, OpenSkyTokenProviderTests covers the cache-and-refresh timing from Step 3, ActiveBoundingBoxRegistryTests covers the subscriber lifecycle from Step 7, and BoundingBoxRequestValidatorTests covers the validation edge cases from Step 8 — together they pin down the behavior across every layer this tour walked through.
tour.languageLesson: NUnit + NSubstitute is used here to mock interfaces like IOpenSkyClient and IAircraftSnapshotProvider, keeping tests fast by never hitting the real OpenSky API.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: lib/main.dart is where the flight ops dashboard boots: it initializes the get_it/injectable dependency injection container, pushes the persisted polling interval to the backend, and mounts the widget tree. It also wires a restart mechanism so settings changes (like switching live-update mode) can rebuild the app cleanly. Everything else in this tour is reachable from here.

tour.title: Tech Stack at a Glance
tour.description: pubspec.yaml declares the dependencies that define this app's architecture before you read a line of Dart: flutter_bloc for state management, go_router for navigation, dio for REST calls, signalr_netcore for real-time push updates, get_it/injectable for DI, and flutter_map for the live map view. Dev dependencies (build_runner, bloc_test, mocktail) hint at a codegen-heavy, test-driven workflow you'll see throughout the tour.
tour.languageLesson: Flutter projects declare all dependencies and dev tooling in a single pubspec.yaml; build_runner-based packages (injectable, json_serializable) require a codegen step that produces the .config.dart and .g.dart files you'll encounter later.

tour.title: App Bootstrap: DI, Routing, Theme
tour.description: Building on main.dart's call to configureDependencies(), injection.dart triggers the generated get_it registration, app_router.dart defines a go_router StatefulShellRoute for the Map/List/Settings tabs plus the detail route, and app_theme.dart builds light/dark ThemeData from the AppColors palette. Together these three files form the 'chrome' every screen in the app runs inside.
tour.languageLesson: AppColors is an immutable model with copyWith and lerp support, letting Flutter's theme system animate smoothly between light and dark palettes rather than snapping instantly.

tour.title: Domain Entities
tour.description: These framework-independent classes define the app's core vocabulary: Aircraft (position, velocity, heading, ICAO24), AircraftSnapshot (a point-in-time list of aircraft plus a staleness flag), BoundingBox (the geographic viewport used to scope queries), and LiveUpdateMode (the enum choosing between polling and realtime dead-reckoning). Every bloc and data source you'll see later speaks in these types.

tour.title: Domain Contracts & Failures
tour.description: AircraftRepository and SettingsRepository are abstract interfaces — contracts the data layer must fulfill without the domain layer knowing anything about Dio, SignalR, or SharedPreferences. Failure is a sealed error taxonomy (network, server, not-found, unknown) and RepositoryException wraps it so errors can be thrown and caught with typed context. This separation is what lets the app swap REST for SignalR under the hood without touching bloc code.
tour.languageLesson: Dart's sealed classes let Failure's subtypes be exhaustively pattern-matched with a switch expression, so the compiler flags any unhandled failure case.

tour.title: REST & SignalR Data Sources
tour.description: This is where the abstract AircraftRepository contract from Step 5 gets a real implementation path. aircraft_remote_data_source.dart is a Dio-based REST client for snapshot/detail fetches, while aircraft_signalr_data_source.dart manages a lazily-established SignalR hub connection with auto-reconnect for live push updates. api_config.dart centralizes the base URL and paths both clients depend on, resolved from a compile-time environment define.

tour.title: DTOs & Repository Implementations
tour.description: aircraft_dto.dart and aircraft_snapshot_dto.dart are json_serializable DTOs that parse raw REST/SignalR payloads and convert them into the domain entities from Step 4. AircraftRepositoryImpl is the concrete class fulfilling the AircraftRepository contract — it switches between SignalR push and REST polling based on the current LiveUpdateMode. SettingsRepositoryImpl mirrors this pattern for user preferences, persisting to SharedPreferences and broadcasting changes as streams.
tour.languageLesson: json_serializable generates a companion .g.dart file with the fromJson factory at build time, so DTOs stay declarative — you annotate the class and never hand-write JSON parsing.

tour.title: ActiveRegionBloc: The Shared State Source
tour.description: ActiveRegionBloc is the upstream bloc every feature screen derives from: it watches AircraftRepository's snapshot stream for the current map/list viewport, restarting the subscription whenever the region changes and preserving stale data across transient errors. Rather than each feature independently polling the repository, MapBloc, AircraftListBloc, and SettingsBloc all subscribe to this single bloc's output — a hub-and-spoke pattern that keeps region-fetching logic in one place.
tour.languageLesson: This is the flutter_bloc part-file convention: the event and state files use 'part of' to attach to the main bloc file, keeping event/state definitions colocated but readable in isolation.

tour.title: Map Feature: Live Visualization
tour.description: MapBloc combines ActiveRegionBloc's region stream (Step 8) with map-only concerns — marker selection, viewport-driven region requests, and retry. map_page.dart is the flutter_map view that renders live aircraft markers, using dead-reckoning interpolation to smoothly animate aircraft positions between polling updates rather than jumping discretely. AircraftMarker renders a rotated SVG icon with separate outline/fill layers so per-aircraft state (airborne/ground/selected) can be tinted without losing the outline.
tour.languageLesson: Dead-reckoning extrapolates an aircraft's next position from its last known velocity and heading, animating between real updates instead of only re-rendering when new data arrives — smoother motion without hammering the API.

tour.title: Aircraft List: Search & Filtering
tour.description: AircraftListBloc derives its state from ActiveRegionBloc's stream (Step 8) plus local search-text events, keeping search filtering and region-fetching as orthogonal concerns. aircraft_list_page.dart renders this as a searchable list with case-insensitive callsign/country matching, a stale-data banner, and tap-through navigation to the detail screen. aircraft_list_skeleton.dart provides a shimmer placeholder during the initial fetch.

tour.title: Aircraft Detail Screen
tour.description: Unlike the region-scoped blocs so far, AircraftDetailBloc is factory-scoped per instance — it fetches a single aircraft's record by ICAO24 and maps success/not-found/error into state independently. aircraft_detail_page.dart composes DataCard, FreshnessIndicator, and RecordRow widgets to display altitude, velocity, and heading, and is reused both as a full-page route and inside the map's bottom sheet (Step 9).

tour.title: Settings Feature
tour.description: SettingsBloc manages theme mode and forwards connection-status updates from ActiveRegionBloc (Step 8), persisting changes through SettingsRepository (Step 7). settings_page.dart lays out theme selection, live-update controls, and connection status; live_updates_block.dart lets the user pick live-update mode and polling interval via a slider with dirty-tracking that restarts the app (echoing main.dart's restart mechanism from Step 1) once saved.

tour.title: Shared UI & App Shell
tour.description: Cross-feature widgets keep the dashboard visually consistent: ConnectivityStateCard is the reusable loading/error/empty card that MapStateOverlay (Step 9) wraps, LiveStatusChip shows an animated flip-counter of live aircraft count, and ShimmerBox provides the shared shimmer-loading mixin used by the list skeleton (Step 10). AppShell wraps go_router's StatefulNavigationShell (Step 3) to render the animated bottom-nav tabs binding Map, List, and Settings together.

tour.title: Test Suite
tour.description: The test suite mirrors lib/'s structure and shows how the clean-architecture split pays off: aircraft_repository_impl_test.dart verifies polling behavior and live-mode switching in isolation using a fake data source, active_region_bloc_test.dart drives the shared bloc from Step 8 through bloc_test's expectLater-style assertions, and map_bloc_test.dart confirms viewport changes forward correctly to ActiveRegionBloc. Because blocs depend on abstract repositories rather than concrete Dio/SignalR clients, every layer from this tour can be tested with mocktail doubles instead of real network calls.
tour.languageLesson: bloc_test's blocTest() helper drives a bloc through a sequence of events and asserts the exact ordered list of emitted states, making bloc behavior verifiable without spinning up widgets.
